domain: wearable sports safety gear, helmet mounts, protective equipment hardware, and adjustment mechanisms

source.domain: wearable sports safety gear, helmet mounts, protective equipment hardware, and adjustment mechanisms

requirements.part_name: Rotating helmet visor quick-lock hinge - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Rotating helmet visor quick-lock hinge'. The
broader use case is: Cycling and skating helmets that need removable or adjustable face shields. The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Detented hinge bracket with integrated latch
tabs. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Allows a visor to pivot between raised and lowered positions while
locking securely under vibration. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Combines rotational clearance, snap-fit retention, asymmetric mounting ears, and repeated small
locking features around a curved helmet interface. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36
or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside
profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are
called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 180 mm x 145 mm x 4 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 5 mm on the length centerline at X=29 mm and X=151 mm, Y=72 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 60 mm long x 13 mm wide through the part, centered at X=90 mm, Y=72 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 45 mm x 48 mm x 1 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Rotating helmet visor quick-lock hinge'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.
requirements.immutable_requirement_note: After review, this English requirement is the product truth for later SubCAD code generation. Do not simplify it to fit current SubCAD limitations.

source.raw_idea_title: Sliding goggle strap anchor for protective helmets

requirements.part_name: Sliding goggle strap anchor for protective helmets - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Sliding goggle strap anchor for protective
helmets'. The broader use case is: Snow, motocross, and field-sport helmets where goggles need
stable strap routing. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Low-profile rail
clip with captive sliding loop. All other product elements are external reference items and must not
be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Captures a goggle strap and lets the anchor
slide to tune strap angle without detaching hardware. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Requires nested sliding geometry, undercuts, strap pass-throughs, stop
features, and a mount that conforms to a curved shell. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM
A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the
outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If
bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 215 mm x 65 mm x 7 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 8 mm on the length centerline at X=13 mm and X=202 mm, Y=32 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 71 mm long x 7 mm wide through the part, centered at X=107 mm, Y=32 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 53 mm x 21 mm x 2 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: integral hook lip
functional_features.description: Leave an integral hook lip on one short end, projecting 8 mm and undercut 6 mm for registration.

Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.

source.domain: wearable sports safety gear, helmet mounts, protective equipment hardware, and adjustment mechanisms

requirements.part_name: Breakaway action camera helmet mount - single metal part

material.reason: low mass with good machinability and stable flat features

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Breakaway action camera helmet mount'. The broader
use case is: Adventure sports helmets that carry accessories but must release during high-force
impacts. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Frangible dovetail base with
replaceable shear insert. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be
modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Holds a camera during normal motion and
separates in a controlled direction during snagging or impact. It is intentionally included in the
SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests representation of intentional weak points, keyed sliding
interfaces, directional release geometry, and replaceable internal inserts. The part is made from
6061-T6 aluminum using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw
oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and
edge treatments into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 140 mm x 90 mm x 42 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 8 mm on the length centerline at X=18 mm and X=122 mm, Y=45 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 46 mm long x 7 mm wide through the part, centered at X=70 mm, Y=45 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 35 mm x 30 mm x 11 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: dovetail slide form
functional_features.description: Machine a straight dovetail groove on the top face, length 116 mm, throat 18 mm, included angle 60 degrees.

source.domain: wearable sports safety gear, helmet mounts, protective equipment hardware, and adjustment mechanisms

requirements.part_name: Indexed chin strap tension dial - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Indexed chin strap tension dial'. The broader use
case is: Helmets and headguards that need fine strap fit adjustment while worn. The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Ratchet dial housing with twin strap spool channels.
All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Takes up slack in paired straps through a dial mechanism with tactile
indexed positions. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Includes
concentric moving parts, pawl-and-tooth detail, strap routing tunnels, finger grip texture, and
serviceable enclosure seams. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends,
bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts
or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 47 mm and length 98 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 15 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 25 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 15 mm wide over 90 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M6 holes through the top flat at X=32 mm and X=65 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 6 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single cylindrical body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'axial through bore' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'end counterbores' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'milled reference flat' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'radial clamp holes' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'split relief slit' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'outside edge treatment' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Round features are coaxial unless the requirement explicitly says they are radial or offset.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Indexed chin strap tension dial'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

source.raw_idea_title: Ventilated shin guard impact insert carrier

requirements.part_name: Ventilated shin guard impact insert carrier - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Ventilated shin guard impact insert carrier'. The
broader use case is: Soccer, hockey, and martial-arts shin guards using interchangeable impact pads.
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Curved perforated carrier shell with
flexible retention lips. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be
modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Holds a removable protective insert while
preserving airflow and keeping the pad aligned against the leg. It is intentionally included in the
SubCAD limit corpus because: Challenges CAD with anatomical curvature, patterned ventilation
cutouts, soft-edge retention flanges, and thin protective shell transitions. The part is made from
low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or
plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into
that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded
pieces.

requirements.part_name: Modular shoulder pad strap junction - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Modular shoulder pad strap junction'. The broader
use case is: Contact-sport shoulder protection where straps connect shell sections across moving
joints. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Multi-axis strap yoke with
pivoting buckle sockets. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be
modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Joins several straps to a protective shell
while allowing limited articulation and quick replacement. It is intentionally included in the
SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests branching load paths, multiple strap slot orientations, pivot
bosses, buckle capture geometry, and compact hardware packaging. The part is made from low-carbon
steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank.
Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same
piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

source.raw_idea_title: Helmet rear fit cradle with flex arms

requirements.part_name: Helmet rear fit cradle with flex arms - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Helmet rear fit cradle with flex arms'. The broader
use case is: Bike, climbing, and skating helmets that tighten around the back of the head. The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Curved occipital cradle with compliant side
arms and adjustment interface. All other product elements are external reference items and must not
be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Supports the rear head fit system and flexes
to accommodate head shape while distributing pressure. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Requires thin compliant arms, ergonomic curvature, integrated hinge-like flex
zones, and attachment points for a separate tension system. The part is made from low-carbon steel,
ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or
plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces,
grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.

requirements.part_name: Protective glove knuckle bridge - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Protective glove knuckle bridge'. The broader use
case is: Goalkeeper, motorcycle, and combat-sport gloves with hard knuckle protection. The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Segmented knuckle bridge with living-link relief
gaps. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Spans across knuckles to absorb impact while allowing finger flexion
beneath the guard. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Exercises
segmented shell modeling, clearance gaps, organic protective contours, underside standoff ribs, and
repeated but non-identical lobes. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent
using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face
all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments
into that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 145 mm x 40 mm x 26 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 8 mm on the length centerline at X=10 mm and X=135 mm, Y=20 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 48 mm long x 16 mm wide through the part, centered at X=72 mm, Y=20 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 36 mm x 18 mm x 8 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

requirements.part_name: Clamp-on face cage repair bracket - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Clamp-on face cage repair bracket'. The broader use
case is: Hockey, lacrosse, and catcher helmets where a face cage attaches to shell hardware. The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Split saddle clamp with shell mounting ear and
anti-rotation groove. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be
modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Clamps around a cage wire and fastens it to a
helmet shell when original tabs are replaced or upgraded. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Tests split-part clamp geometry, cylindrical wire capture, screw boss
placement, anti-rotation features, and mixed curved-flat mating surfaces. The part is made from 1045
medium-carbon steel, normalized using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate
blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that
same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded
pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 135 mm x 60 mm x 6 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 6 mm on the length centerline at X=12 mm and X=123 mm, Y=30 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 45 mm long x 10 mm wide through the part, centered at X=67 mm, Y=30 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 33 mm x 20 mm x 3 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: side tapped hole
functional_features.description: Tap one side hole M7 from the right long edge into the central pocket; hole axis is parallel to Y.

requirements.part_name: Pull-tab release buckle guard - single metal part

material.reason: corrosion resistance and cleanable metal surfaces

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Pull-tab release buckle guard'. The broader use
case is: Protective vests, pads, and helmets where buckles must be accessible but shielded from
accidental release. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Buckle shroud with
guarded pull-tab channel. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be
modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Covers a quick-release buckle while leaving a
raised pull tab accessible to a gloved hand. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Combines protective hooding, ergonomic access openings, strap clearance slots, flexible
latch geometry, and collision-sensitive internal spaces. The part is made from AISI 316 stainless
steel using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile,
machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called
out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
